heading: Consolidation and reform of Federal milk marketing orders
label: 7253 Consolidation and reform of Federal milk marketing orders
notes: Amendments2014—Subsec. (a)(2). inserted at end “Subsection (b) does not apply to the authority of the Secretary under this subsection.”Pub. L. 113–79

Use of Option 1A as Price Structure for Class I Milk Under Consolidated Federal Milk Marketing Orders, , , 1501A–517, provided that:Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(8) [§ 1]113 Stat. 1536Nov. 29, 1999“(a).—Final Rule DefinedIn this section, the term ‘final rule’ means the final rule for the consolidation and reform of Federal milk marketing orders that was published in the Federal Register on (64 Fed. Reg. 47897–48021), to comply with section 143 of the Federal Agriculture Improvement and Reform Act of 1996 ().September 1, 19997 U.S.C. 7253“(b).—Implementation of Final Rule for Milk Order ReformSubject to subsection (c), the final rule shall take effect, and be implemented by the Secretary of Agriculture, on the first day of the first month beginning at least 30 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 29, 1999“(c)1A .—Use of Optionfor Pricing Class I MilkIn lieu of the Class I price differentials specified in the final rule, the Secretary of Agriculture shall price fluid or Class I milk under the Federal milk marketing orders using the Class I price differentials identified as Option 1A ‘Location-Specific Differentials Analysis’ in the proposed rule published in the Federal Register on (63 Fed. Reg. 4802, 4809), except that the Secretary shall include the corrections and modifications to such Class I differentials made by the Secretary through .January 30, 1998April 2, 1999“(d).—Effect of Prior Announcement of Minimum PricesIf the Secretary of Agriculture announces minimum prices for milk under Federal milk marketing orders pursuant to section 1000.50 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations, before the effective date specified in subsection (b), the minimum prices so announced before that date shall be the only applicable minimum prices under Federal milk marketing orders for the month or months for which the prices have been announced.“(e).—Implementation of RequirementThe implementation of the final rule, as modified by subsection (c), shall not be subject to any of the following:“(1)The notice and hearing requirements of section 8c(3) of the Agricultural Adjustment Act (), reenacted with amendments by the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937, or the notice and comment provisions of , United States Code.7 U.S.C. 608c(3)section 553 of title 5“(2)A referendum conducted by the Secretary of Agriculture pursuant to subsections (17) or (19) of section 8c of the Agricultural Adjustment Act (), reenacted with amendments by the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937.7 U.S.C. 608c“(3)The Statement of Policy of the Secretary of Agriculture effective (36 Fed. Reg. 13804), relating to notices of proposed rulemaking and public participation in rulemaking.July 24, 1971“(4)Chapter 35 of title 44, United States Code (commonly known as the Paperwork Reduction Act).“(5)Any decision, restraining order, or injunction issued by a United States court before the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Nov. 29, 1999

Further Rulemaking To Develop Pricing Methods for Class III and Class IV Milk Under Marketing Orders, , , 1501A–518, provided that:Pub. L. 106–113, div. B, § 1000(a)(8) [§ 2]113 Stat. 1536Nov. 29, 1999“(a).—Congressional FindingThe Class III and Class IV milk pricing formulas included in the final decision for the consolidation and reform of Federal milk marketing orders, as published in the Federal Register on (64 Fed. Reg. 16025), do not adequately reflect public comment on the original proposed rule published in the Federal Register on (63 Fed. Reg. 4802), and are sufficiently different from the proposed rule and any comments submitted with regard to the proposed rule that further emergency rulemaking is merited.April 2, 1999January 30, 1998“(b).—Rulemaking RequiredThe Secretary of Agriculture shall conduct rulemaking, on the record after an opportunity for an agency hearing, to reconsider the Class III and Class IV milk pricing formulas included in the final rule for the consolidation and reform of Federal milk marketing orders that was published in the Federal Register on (64 Fed. Reg. 47897–48021).September 1, 1999“(c).—Time Period for RulemakingOn , the Secretary of Agriculture shall publish in the Federal Register a final decision on the Class III and Class IV milk pricing formulas. The resulting formulas shall take effect, and be implemented by the Secretary, on .December 1, 2000January 1, 2001“(d).—Effect of Court OrderThe actions authorized by subsections (b) and (c) are intended to ensure the timely publication and implementation of new pricing formulas for Class III and Class IV milk. In the event that the Secretary of Agriculture is enjoined or otherwise restrained by a court order from implementing a final decision within the time period specified in subsection (c), the length of time for which that injunction or other restraining order is effective shall be added to the time limitations specified in subsection (c) thereby extending those time limitations by a period of time equal to the period of time for which the injunction or other restraining order is effective.“(e).—Failure To Timely Complete RulemakingIf the Secretary of Agriculture fails to implement new Class III and Class IV milk pricing formulas within the time period required under subsection (c) (plus any additional period provided under subsection (d)), the Secretary may not assess or collect assessments from milk producers or handlers under section 8c of the Agricultural Adjustment Act (), reenacted with amendments by the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937, for marketing order administration and services provided under such section after the end of that period until the pricing formulas are implemented. The Secretary may not reduce the level of services provided under that section on account of the prohibition against assessments, but shall rather cover the cost of marketing order administration and services through funds available for the Agricultural Marketing Service of the Department.7 U.S.C. 608c“(f).—Implementation of RequirementThe implementation of the final decision on new Class III and Class IV milk pricing formulas shall not be subject to congressional review under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code.”
amendments: Amendments2014—Subsec. (a)(2). inserted at end “Subsection (b) does not apply to the authority of the Secretary under this subsection.”Pub. L. 113–79

children.heading: Required consolidation
children.label: 1 Required consolidation
children.text: The Secretary shall amend Federal milk marketing orders issued under to limit the number of Federal milk marketing orders to not less than 10 and not more than 14 orders.section 608c of this title

children.heading: Inclusion of California as separate order
children.label: 2 Inclusion of California as separate order
children.text: Upon the petition and approval of California dairy producers in the manner provided in , the Secretary shall designate the State of California as a separate Federal milk marketing order. The order covering California shall have the right to reblend and distribute order receipts to recognize quota value. Subsection (b) does not apply to the authority of the Secretary under this subsection.section 608c of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The use of utilization rates and multiple basing points for the pricing of fluid milk.

children.label: B
children.text: The use of uniform multiple component pricing when developing 1 or more basic formula prices for manufacturing milk.

children.heading: Effect of existing law
children.label: 4 Effect of existing law
children.text: In implementing the consolidation of Federal milk marketing orders and related reforms under this subsection, the Secretary may not consider, or base any decision on, the table contained in .section 608c(5)(A) of this title

children.heading: Use of informal rulemaking
children.label: 1 Use of informal rulemaking
children.text: To implement the consolidation of Federal milk marketing orders and related reforms under subsection (a), the Secretary shall use the notice and comment procedures provided in .section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Proposed amendments
children.label: A Proposed amendments
children.text: The Secretary shall announce the proposed amendments to be made under subsection (a) not later than 2 years after .April 4, 1996

children.heading: Final amendments
children.label: B Final amendments
children.text: The Secretary shall implement the amendments not later than 3 years after .April 4, 1996

children.heading: Effect of court order
children.label: 3 Effect of court order
children.text: The actions authorized by this subsection are intended to ensure the timely publication and implementation of new and amended Federal milk marketing orders. In the event that the Secretary is enjoined or otherwise restrained by a court order from publishing or implementing the consolidation and related reforms under subsection (a), the length of time for which that injunction or other restraining order is effective shall be added to the time limitations specified in paragraph (2) thereby extending those time limitations by a period of time equal to the period of time for which the injunction or other restraining order is effective.

children.heading: Failure to timely consolidate orders
children.label: c Failure to timely consolidate orders
children.text: If the Secretary fails to implement the consolidation required under subsection (a)(1) within the time period required under subsection (b)(2)(B) (plus any additional period provided under subsection (b)(3)), the Secretary may not assess or collect assessments from milk producers or handlers under such for marketing order administration and services provided under such section after the end of that period until the consolidation is completed. The Secretary may not reduce the level of services provided under the section on account of the prohibition against assessments, but shall rather cover the cost of marketing order administration and services through funds available for the Agricultural Marketing Service of the Department.section 608c of this title

children.label: A
children.text: reviewing the Federal milk marketing order system established pursuant to in light of the reforms required by subsection (a);section 608c of this title

children.label: B
children.text: describing the efforts underway and the progress made in implementing the reforms required by subsection (a); and

children.label: C
children.text: containing such recommendations as the Secretary considers appropriate for further improvements and reforms to the Federal milk marketing order system.

children.heading: Effect of other laws
children.label: 2 Effect of other laws
children.text: Any limitation imposed by Act of Congress on the conduct or completion of reports to Congress shall not apply to the report required under this section, unless the limitation specifically refers to this section.
